text: Secure web development is essential to protect user data and maintain trust. First, HTTPS encrypts traffic between the browser and the server, preventing eavesdropping. Next, strong authentication methods like passwords and two-factor verification ensure only authorised users gain access. Input validation is equally critical—never trust user input without checking it against expected formats, as this prevents attacks like SQL injection. Common vulnerabilities include cross-site scripting (XSS) and broken authentication. Regularly updating software and logging suspicious activity also help. By implementing these practices, developers minimise risks and create safer online experiences for everyone.
